name: Espresso-Chocoloate Fudge
recipeIngredient: 6 ounces semisweet or bittersweet chocolate (not unsweetened)
recipeInstructions:
1. Line an 8-inch square glass baking dish with foil, overlapping sides.
2. Combine bittersweet chocolate, marshmallow cream, unsweetened chocolate, and vanilla in a medium bowl.
3. Mix water and espresso powder in a heavy large saucepan until the espresso powder dissolves.
4. Add sugar, sweetened condensed milk, whipping cream, and unsalted butter to the saucepan. Stir constantly but slowly with a wooden spoon until the candy thermometer registers 234°F, about 12 minutes.
5. Pour the hot mixture into the bowl of chocolate ingredients (do not scrape the pan).
6. Stir the mixture vigorously with a wooden spoon until all the chocolate melts and the fudge thickens slightly, about 3 minutes.
7. Transfer the fudge mixture to the prepared baking dish and smooth the top with a rubber spatula.
8. Refrigerate the fudge uncovered until it is firm enough to cut, about 2 hours.
9. Using aluminum foil as an aid, lift the fudge from the pan. Trim the edges of the fudge.
10. Cut into 30 pieces.
11. Melt white chocolate in a double boiler over simmering water.
12. Drizzle the melted white chocolate decoratively over the fudge using a fork.
13. Press one espresso bean onto the top of each piece of fudge.
14. Place the fudge in candy paper or foil cups and refrigerate until the white chocolate sets, about 20 minutes.
15. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator. Bring to room temperature before serving.
16. For holiday gift giving, line small boxes with holiday paper and tell recipients to refrigerate any leftovers.
